title: Security and Oversight Get Sharper
description: We tightened login protection, expanded audit visibility, and improved request oversight so growing Filipino businesses can run payroll operations with more trust and less guesswork.
body: For many Filipino business owners, the hardest backoffice problems are not always dramatic ones. They show up when the wrong person might get into the system, when a sensitive action is hard to trace, or when a request sits in limbo without enough visibility. As teams grow, those small operational cracks become expensive because payroll and HR work depend on trust, timing, and accountability. A platform starts to feel truly dependable when it protects access and makes important activity easier to review. This update is about strengthening those everyday controls so YAHSHUA One feels safer and more credible in real business use.

Yesterday we pushed a set of improvements that make the product more disciplined behind the scenes and easier to trust in practice. We strengthened login protection so account access is handled with tighter checks, which helps reduce the chances of misuse before it becomes a bigger problem. We also moved audit visibility forward, giving payroll officers and admins a clearer way to review what has been happening inside the system without relying on fragmented follow-up. On top of that, we improved the request experience around post-dated check workflows by giving teams a more dedicated place to track and manage those actions. In plain language, this means safer entry, better visibility, and a cleaner operational view for the people responsible for sensitive work.

What this means for you is a platform that is steadily earning trust where it matters most. Your team gets better protection at login, better visibility into important activity, and a more organized way to stay on top of request-driven processes. That lowers the risk of confusion, reduces dependence on side conversations, and makes it easier to run payroll and HR operations with confidence. It also moves YAHSHUA One closer to being a complete payroll and HR backoffice, where security, oversight, and day-to-day workflows all support each other instead of living in separate systems. We are continuing to build toward a product that helps Filipino businesses operate with stronger controls without making the work feel heavier.

title: Safer Access, Cleaner Payroll Corrections
description: We strengthened account security, improved how payroll corrections work after a period is closed, and made request and reporting flows easier to trust as YAHSHUA One grows into a more complete backoffice platform.
body: For many Filipino business owners, backoffice stress does not only come from calculating payroll correctly. It also comes from what happens around the edges: who is allowed to get in, what happens when a payroll period is already closed, and how teams handle last-minute changes without creating confusion. A system starts to feel truly reliable when it can protect access, prevent unsafe edits, and still give operators a clean way to fix legitimate issues. That matters even more as companies grow and more people touch payroll, HR requests, and internal records every day. This update is about making YAHSHUA One feel safer and more operationally mature, not just more feature-rich.

Yesterday we pushed several practical improvements that move the product in that direction. We strengthened account access so login checks are stricter and easier to trust, helping make sure the right people get through the door and the wrong ones do not. We also improved how payroll corrections can be handled after a period has already been finalized, which means businesses get a cleaner path for resolving changes without reopening everything in a messy way. On top of that, request workflows around post-dated check changes and division-related payroll actions became more disciplined, with better guardrails when a payroll period is locked and better handling when a request needs to be withdrawn or moved through multiple approval steps. We also tightened reporting behavior so long pages are easier to navigate and review, which sounds small but matters when teams are working through real payroll data under time pressure.

What this means for you is a platform that keeps getting stronger in the places where trust is won or lost. Your team gets better protection around account access, safer handling for sensitive payroll changes, and cleaner request flows when real-life situations force adjustments. That reduces the chances of avoidable mistakes, weak controls, or awkward workarounds that usually appear once a business starts scaling. It also moves YAHSHUA One closer to being a complete payroll and HR backoffice, where security, approvals, corrections, and day-to-day usability all work together instead of living in separate tools and side processes. We are continuing to build toward a system that helps Filipino businesses operate with more confidence, even when payroll and people workflows get complicated.

title: Employee Self-Service Steps Forward
description: We pushed YAHSHUA One's employee self-service experience forward with a more dedicated portal flow, easier employee-filed leave requests, and better visibility into leave activity for the teams managing it.
body: For many Filipino business owners, payroll and HR friction does not only happen inside the admin team. It also shows up when employees have to keep asking HR for simple things they should be able to do themselves. Filing leave, checking requests, or accessing a basic employee portal should not feel like a manual workaround in 2026. When those everyday actions stay clunky, HR becomes a bottleneck and employees feel like the system was not really built for them. This update matters because a modern backoffice platform has to serve not just managers and payroll admins, but the workforce using it day to day.

Yesterday's progress pushed that employee side of YAHSHUA One further into real usability. We continued building a more dedicated employee self-service portal experience, including the kiosk and portal flows that give employees a clearer place to interact with the system. We also moved employee-filed leave applications forward, which means the platform is getting better at letting staff submit their own leave requests without depending on back-and-forth manual encoding. On the management side, leave monitoring is also becoming more complete, so teams can review and track leave activity more clearly from the admin view. We also pushed forward a cancellation and withdrawal workflow for certain request types, which matters because real businesses need a clean way to reverse or correct requests when plans change.

What this means for you is a product that is steadily becoming more complete on both sides of the experience. Employees get closer to a cleaner self-service flow, while HR and payroll teams get better oversight of the requests coming in. That reduces repetitive admin work, shortens turnaround time, and helps everyone interact with the system in a more natural way. It also moves YAHSHUA One closer to being a full payroll and HR backoffice, where employee actions, approvals, and operational visibility all connect properly in one place. We are building toward a platform that does not just help you process internal work faster, but also gives your people a smoother and more trustworthy way to handle their side of the process.

title: Budgets Meet Real Payroll Visibility
description: We added budget management and budget-versus-actual reporting so businesses can finally compare payroll plans against what is really happening, while also tightening access to sensitive payroll reports.
body: For many Filipino business owners, payroll and HR pressure does not end once a salary is computed correctly. The harder question usually comes after that: are we still operating within the budget we planned, and where are the gaps starting to show? When that visibility is missing, leaders end up bouncing between spreadsheets, exports, and gut feel just to understand whether labor costs are still under control. That makes planning slower, decisions riskier, and month-end conversations more stressful than they need to be. This update matters because YAHSHUA One is starting to close that gap between payroll activity and financial clarity.

Yesterday we moved budget management forward in a big way inside the product. In plain language, the system is gaining a proper place to create and manage budgets, along with a clear report that compares what you planned against what actually happened. That means teams can review variances without piecing the story together by hand from separate tools. We also pushed the user experience around these budget screens so the workflow feels practical instead of half-built. On top of that, we tightened access around at least one payroll report area so the right information stays visible to the right people and not everyone by default.

What this means for you is a backoffice platform that is getting smarter about both control and decision-making. You are not only moving toward accurate payroll processing, but toward a system that helps you see whether your people costs are lining up with the business plan. That kind of visibility makes it easier to catch issues earlier, explain results more confidently, and operate with less spreadsheet guesswork. It also brings YAHSHUA One closer to being a complete payroll and HR backoffice, where planning, reporting, permissions, and execution all live together in one place. We are building toward a product that does not just record what happened, but helps business owners lead with better visibility before small variances become expensive surprises.

title: Payroll Imports Get More Accurate, Leave Permissions Get Clearer
description: We improved payroll imports so daily pay calculations stay aligned with each company's setup, and we added clearer leave approval controls so the right people can review and approve each leave type.
body: Yesterday's work focused on two practical things that help teams trust the system more in daily use: cleaner payroll imports and clearer leave permissions. These may not be flashy headline features, but they solve the kinds of small friction points that slow teams down when payroll and leave processes need to move quickly and correctly.

On the payroll side, YAHSHUA One now handles imported calculator records more accurately by respecting each company's own working-day setup when figuring out daily rates. In plain language, that means less manual correction when bringing payroll data into the system. On the people-management side, admins can now set leave-type authorities more clearly, including who can review, recommend, approve, or simply view each kind of leave request.

What this means for you is a product that keeps getting safer and smoother where it counts. Payroll imports become more dependable, and leave requests are easier to route to the right people without confusion. It is another steady step toward a backoffice platform that feels reliable not just in demos, but in real day-to-day operations.

title: Payroll Visibility Gets Deeper
description: We shipped a major wave of payroll reports, employee self-service payroll access, and bulk payroll workflow improvements that help growing businesses see more, do more, and rely less on manual work.
body: For many Filipino business owners, payroll stress does not only come from calculations. It also comes from not being able to see the right reports fast enough, answer employee questions clearly, or handle growing payroll complexity without adding more manual work. As teams grow, leaders need better visibility into status changes, cash advances, final pay, annual planning, attendance breakdowns, and the overall story behind payroll activity. Employees also expect a more modern experience where they can see their own records instead of chasing HR for every small request. This update matters because a true backoffice platform needs to serve both the operators running payroll and the people affected by it every payday.

Yesterday we shipped a broad set of improvements that make YAHSHUA One more complete and much more practical in daily use. We added more payroll report coverage, including employee status reports, cash advance reporting, attendance breakdown views, final pay visibility, annual planning support, and summary pages that help admins understand what is happening across the business. We also expanded employee self-service capabilities so workers can access their payslips, payroll history, deductions, certificates, and several application flows more directly inside the system. On top of that, we improved bulk payroll-related workflows, including time uploads with flexible column mapping and stronger support for employees who work across multiple assignments. In plain language, this means less spreadsheet wrangling, fewer repetitive HR follow-ups, and a cleaner path from raw payroll activity to useful action.

What this means for you is a payroll and HR backoffice that is getting more usable from every angle. Business owners and admins can review more of the reports they need without depending on scattered exports or developer help, while employees gain better access to their own payroll information and request flows. That reduces friction across the whole company because the system starts answering more questions on its own. It also moves YAHSHUA One closer to being a complete operating layer for payroll and HR, where reporting, employee access, and operational control all live together in one place. We are continuing to build toward a product that does not just process payroll correctly, but helps businesses run with more clarity, speed, and confidence.

title: More Reports, Better Access Control
description: We added clearer payroll reporting, stronger privacy controls for sensitive employee information, and better admin tools so teams can run payroll and backoffice work with more confidence.
body: Yesterday's work pushed YAHSHUA One forward in a few practical ways that business teams will actually feel. We improved payroll reporting so admins can review important summaries more clearly, expanded employee and company management tools, and tightened who can view sensitive information inside the system. In plain language: the platform is getting easier to use while also getting safer.

We also continued strengthening the admin side of the product with better access controls, cleaner setup pages, and more help documentation for payroll and accounting workflows. That means less guesswork for teams, better protection for private records, and a smoother path toward running more of your operations in one place.

title: Reports and Controls Get Real
description: We shipped a strong mix of payroll reporting, tighter access controls, and smarter accounting foundations so business owners can see more clearly, operate more safely, and rely less on manual patchwork.
body: For many Filipino business owners, growth creates two kinds of pressure at the same time: you need clearer reports to understand what is happening, and you need tighter controls so the wrong people do not touch the wrong data. When those two things are missing, teams end up juggling spreadsheets, messaging screenshots, and relying on memory just to answer simple operational questions. That slows down payroll, weakens accountability, and makes backoffice work feel heavier than it should. Yesterday's progress mattered because it focused on visibility and control, which are two of the biggest ingredients of a platform you can actually trust as your company gets more complex. These are the kinds of improvements that make a system feel less like a prototype and more like a serious operating tool for real businesses in the Philippines.

We shipped new payroll reporting pages that make it easier to review annual plans, attendance breakdowns, and standard output formats teams often need when coordinating operations. In plain language, that means business owners and admins are getting more ways to see the story behind their workforce data without needing a developer to dig it out for them. We also strengthened who can see sensitive employee information by enforcing confidentiality rules based on the viewer's assigned level, which helps protect private data inside the system. On the accounting side, a wide set of management pages and core controls continued moving forward, covering practical things like customer types, payment types, vendor types, units of measure, purchase orders, debit memo behavior, attachment security, and settings that shape how transactions are organized. None of that is flashy for the sake of flash, but it is exactly the kind of infrastructure that makes a backoffice platform cleaner, safer, and easier to run every day.

What this means for you is a more usable YAHSHUA One experience from two directions at once: better visibility into operations and better protection around important business data. Teams can make decisions faster when reports are easier to access, and they can work with more confidence when permissions and record controls are handled properly inside the product. It also means less dependence on side systems, manual follow-up, or tribal knowledge just to keep payroll and accounting workflows moving. Every one of these updates helps YAHSHUA One grow into a complete payroll and HR backoffice, where reporting, permissions, and operational setup all live together in one dependable place. We are continuing to build toward a platform that does not just process work, but helps businesses lead with more clarity, control, and confidence.

title: Secure Device Access Gets Simpler
description: We completed a cleaner, more secure way for timekeeping and kiosk devices to connect to YAHSHUA One, making hardware setup easier to trust as businesses scale their attendance operations.
body: For many Filipino business owners, attendance hardware is only helpful when it works reliably in the real world. A timekeeping device at the entrance, a kiosk on the floor, or a shared station for employee check-ins can quickly become a headache if connection and security are shaky. When devices are difficult to authorize, teams fall back to manual workarounds, and that creates room for delays, confusion, and avoidable errors. That problem gets worse as a company adds more people, more locations, or more devices across operations. This update matters because a serious payroll and HR platform needs secure device access that feels practical, not fragile.

Yesterday we finished a token-based authentication flow for timekeeping and kiosk devices inside YAHSHUA One. In plain language, that means devices now have a clearer and safer way to prove that they are allowed to connect to your system. Instead of relying on looser access patterns, the platform can now recognize approved devices more cleanly and reduce the risk of the wrong hardware connecting where it should not. This is the kind of work users may not see as a flashy screen change, but it directly improves how dependable the product feels when connected to real attendance tools. It also gives us a stronger base for device-driven workflows that businesses will depend on every single day.

What this means for you is more confidence that the bridge between physical attendance devices and your backoffice system is getting stronger. Businesses using kiosks or timekeeping tools will be better positioned to scale without feeling like each new device introduces new risk. It moves YAHSHUA One closer to being a complete payroll and HR backoffice because attendance capture is not a side feature; it is one of the first points where operational truth enters the system. When that entry point is more secure and easier to manage, everything downstream becomes more trustworthy too. We are continuing to tighten the foundation so the platform can support not just software workflows, but the real devices and daily routines that power a growing company.

title: Admin Setup, Payroll Rules, and Device Access Got Better
description: We made it easier to manage employee details, set minimum wage rules inside the platform, and securely connect timekeeping devices — while also fixing backend issues so the product feels more reliable day to day.
body: Yesterday's work focused on the kind of improvements that make a business platform feel more usable in real life. We cleaned up important employee and company information, added better support for minimum wage settings, and tightened how timekeeping and kiosk devices connect securely to the system. We also fixed backend migration issues that help the product behave more reliably behind the scenes.

For early supporters, the simple version is this: YAHSHUA One is getting stronger in the practical details that teams deal with every day. It is becoming easier to keep employee records accurate, apply the right payroll rules, and trust that connected devices and admin tools will work the way they should. Not flashy for the sake of flash — just steady progress toward a backoffice platform businesses can actually depend on.

title: Core Admin Work Gets Real
description: We shipped a major wave of admin and payroll foundation work that makes YAHSHUA One easier to manage, safer to operate, and more complete for real business use.
body: For Filipino business owners, backoffice pain rarely comes from one dramatic problem. It usually comes from dozens of small gaps that slow your team down every day, like settings pages that are missing, imports that are hard to track, or company rules that still live in scattered spreadsheets. Those gaps create friction at exactly the wrong time, especially when payroll, HR, and admin work need to move quickly and cleanly. As a company grows, even simple tasks become risky when the system cannot fully reflect how the business actually operates. This update matters because it closes more of those gaps and pushes YAHSHUA One closer to being a platform you can truly run your operations on.

Yesterday we completed a broad set of product improvements across the admin and payroll foundation of YAHSHUA One. In plain language, we made the system better at managing the lists, settings, and supporting records that businesses rely on behind the scenes, including items like location-based minimum wage setup, inventory-related settings, posting keys, camp codes, geofences, salary status ranks, and import workflows. We also added stronger support for tracking import jobs, which gives teams a clearer way to see what was uploaded and what happened after the upload. On the payroll side, we improved the platform's ability to handle automatic email memo settings and device authentication for timekeeping tools, which helps connect day-to-day operations more smoothly. We also tightened delete and deactivate behavior in key settings areas, so teams can clean up records with more confidence and fewer unsafe workarounds.

What this means for you is that YAHSHUA One is becoming much more usable in the messy real world of running a business. More of the details that normally get pushed into side spreadsheets, manual follow-up, or developer-only fixes can now live properly inside the platform where they belong. That makes setup cleaner, day-to-day admin work faster, and payroll-related operations easier to trust as your business gets more complex. It also gives us a stronger foundation for the bigger workflows still ahead, because reliable payroll and HR software is built on complete settings, clear records, and safer controls. We are not just adding features for show. We are steadily turning YAHSHUA One into a complete payroll and HR backoffice that feels ready for real companies, not just demos.

title: Billing Pages Get Clearer and Access Feels Smoother
description: We improved how companies view invoice details, tightened access flow behavior, and cleaned up parts of the interface so YAHSHUA One feels more reliable and easier to navigate day to day.
body: As a product gets more serious, the little moments start to matter more. It is not only about whether a feature exists, but whether the right people can get to the right page at the right time, and whether what they see is clear enough to act on without second-guessing. For growing teams handling payroll, billing, and admin work, friction usually shows up in these exact places: missing details, confusing access behavior, or an interface that feels slightly off when you are trying to move fast. Yesterday's work focused on reducing that friction so the product feels steadier in real daily use.

We improved the staff billing area so invoice pages now show more of the details teams actually need when reviewing charges and account activity. In plain language, that means less clicking around and less guessing when you need to understand what an invoice is for. We also tightened how access-aware routing works across the app, which helps make sure people land in the right place based on what they are allowed to see. On top of that, we restored parts of the interface that had been lost and cleaned up layout spacing, which makes the product feel more consistent and less brittle as you move through it.

For early supporters, this is the kind of progress that matters more than flashy demos. YAHSHUA One is getting better at the everyday experience of running a real backoffice platform: seeing the right information, reaching the right screens, and trusting the interface not to get in your way. These improvements make billing and admin workflows easier to follow now, while also strengthening the overall product foundation for the bigger payroll, HR, and finance capabilities we are continuing to build. We are still shipping fast, but just as importantly, we are making the experience cleaner, clearer, and more dependable with each pass.

title: Smarter Payroll Memos, Finally Automated
description: We shipped automated payroll memo email settings and a dedicated memo response settings page, making it easier for businesses to send the right payroll communications without chasing manual follow-ups.
body: For many Filipino business owners, payroll is not only about getting numbers right but also about getting communication right. A payroll memo that arrives late, goes to the wrong place, or gets lost in manual follow-up creates confusion that quickly turns into stress for both managers and employees. When teams are already juggling cutoff deadlines, approvals, and compliance tasks, even a simple memo workflow can become another avoidable headache. That is especially true for growing businesses that do not have a large HR team watching every small detail. This update matters because it helps payroll communication become more reliable, more consistent, and less dependent on someone remembering to do everything by hand.

Yesterday we completed two connected improvements that make this workflow far more usable. First, YAHSHUA One can now support automatic email memo settings behind the scenes, which means the platform is better prepared to send payroll-related memo communications on schedule instead of relying on manual sending every time. Second, we added a dedicated settings page for memo responses, so teams now have a clearer place to manage how these communications are handled inside the product. In plain language, this means less hunting around, less guesswork, and a more organized setup for a process that is easy to overlook until it becomes urgent. It is not a flashy headline feature, but it is exactly the kind of practical work that makes a backoffice platform feel mature and dependable in daily operations.

What this means for you is that YAHSHUA One is getting better at handling the small but critical communication steps that surround payroll. Businesses will be able to manage payroll memo behavior with more confidence, reduce manual reminders, and create a cleaner experience for the people receiving these notices. That helps teams stay aligned without adding extra admin work every cutoff period. It also moves the platform closer to becoming a complete payroll and HR backoffice, where both the core computations and the surrounding workflows are handled in one place. We are continuing to build toward a system that does not just process payroll, but also supports the real operational follow-through that makes payroll work smoothly in the real world.

title: Payroll Engine Crosses a Major Threshold
description: We shipped a huge wave of payroll engine capabilities, editing tools, audit visibility, and testing work that brings YAHSHUA One much closer to a payroll and HR backoffice Filipino businesses can truly rely on.
body: For Filipino business owners, payroll is not just a calculation problem but a trust problem. Every deduction, contribution, override, and correction has to make sense when your team asks questions and when government rules come into play. A system can look modern on the surface and still fall apart if the engine underneath is too rigid, too opaque, or too fragile for real business conditions. That is why this stage of work matters so much. We focused on making the payroll core more real, more explainable, and more dependable for the messy situations that happen in actual operations.

Yesterday we shipped a major block of work that strengthens both the engine itself and the experience around it. YAHSHUA One can now handle more of the real logic behind payroll runs, including how pay components are defined, how formulas are managed, how lookup tables are maintained, and how statutory rules are loaded and applied with greater confidence. We also improved the way teams can review changes before publishing them, inspect version history, drill into audit details, and make controlled corrections for already closed periods. On the front end, the payroll engine pages and editors became far more usable, so admins are not stuck relying on hidden setup or developer intervention just to shape how payroll behaves. On top of that, a serious layer of end-to-end testing and validation was completed so this does not just work in theory, but holds up under more realistic use.

What this means for you is that YAHSHUA One is moving beyond being a promising payroll product and toward becoming a true operating system for payroll and HR backoffice work. It gives growing businesses a clearer way to define how compensation is computed, see the impact of changes before they go live, and understand why a result came out the way it did. That reduces fear, guesswork, and spreadsheet dependency, which are usually the things that slow teams down as payroll gets more complex. It also lays stronger groundwork for the next layers of the product, from approvals and reporting to AI assistance and compliance workflows. We are still building fast, but more importantly, we are building the parts that make the platform feel trustworthy when real salaries, real rules, and real people are involved.

title: Theo Gets Better Controls and Clearer Usage Tracking
description: We made it easier for teams to control how Theo is used, see how heavily it is being used, and manage more of their company setup directly inside YAHSHUA One.
body: Yesterday's work focused on making Theo feel less like a hidden experiment and more like a feature your team can confidently manage. Early on, AI features can be exciting but still feel a little black-box if business owners do not know who can access them, how often they are being used, or what settings are in place behind the scenes. That uncertainty matters, especially for payroll and backoffice work where control and trust are everything. We wanted Theo to feel more visible, more intentional, and easier to govern as companies grow. This update is a step in that direction.

You can now expect better controls around how Theo is enabled and managed inside the platform. In plain language, that means businesses are getting clearer settings for turning Theo on, deciding who should be allowed to use it, and shaping how it behaves in day-to-day operations. We also improved visibility into Theo conversations, so teams can better understand how much it is being used instead of guessing in the dark. On top of that, more company setup details can now be managed directly in the system, which helps reduce manual patchwork and makes the product feel more complete. None of this is flashy for the sake of flash, but it makes the platform more trustworthy and more usable for real teams.

For early supporters, the takeaway is simple: YAHSHUA One is getting sharper not just in what it can do, but in how safely and clearly it can be rolled out inside a business. AI features are only useful if admins can actually control them and teams can understand their impact, and we are building toward that standard from the start. We are also continuing to close practical setup gaps so companies can manage more of their operating details in one place. That means less duct tape, fewer blind spots, and a smoother path from experimentation to daily use. The product keeps getting more serious in the right ways.

title: Payroll Engine Gets Real
description: We shipped a major step toward a truly reliable Philippine payroll core, while also filling in key settings and visibility tools that make the platform more complete for real business use.
body: For a Filipino business owner, payroll is where trust either grows or breaks. It is not enough for a system to look polished on the surface if the actual computation underneath is still fragile, incomplete, or too rigid for real-world company rules. The hard part is making sure every deduction, contribution, tax rule, and pay scenario behaves consistently when your team starts depending on it every cutoff. At the same time, the surrounding admin setup has to be complete, because even a strong engine becomes frustrating if the settings around it are full of gaps. Yesterday's work mattered because it pushed YAHSHUA One deeper into that serious backoffice territory where the foundation starts matching the ambition.

We built out a much stronger payroll computation core behind the scenes. In plain language, that means the system is getting better at taking your payroll rules, applying them in the right order, and producing pay results that are easier to trust and explain. We also expanded the settings side of the platform so more payroll-related company data can now be created, updated, and managed properly instead of being handled through workarounds or missing flows. On top of that, Theo now gives better visibility into usage and settings, which helps teams understand how the built-in AI assistant is being used and controlled. None of this is flashy for its own sake, but it is exactly the kind of work that turns a promising product into one that can carry real operations.

What this means for you is simple: YAHSHUA One is getting closer to becoming a payroll and HR backoffice you can actually run a business on, not just test in a demo. The engine is becoming more capable of handling the messy logic that Philippine payroll demands, while the admin side is becoming more complete and less dependent on manual fixes. That gives your team a cleaner path from setup, to computation, to review, to day-to-day use. It also means every new layer we add from approvals to reports to AI assistance now has a stronger base to stand on. We are still building fast, but more importantly, we are building the parts that make the platform dependable when the stakes are real.

title: Smarter Payment Account Controls
description: We completed a key payment settings improvement so businesses can now match the right accounting accounts to the right payment types more clearly and with less manual cleanup.
body: As Filipino business owners grow, payroll and payments get more complicated fast. It is no longer enough to simply release money on time because every payment also needs to land in the right accounting bucket. Salaries, reimbursements, advances, and other payment types each tell a different financial story, and mixing them together creates confusion later. That confusion usually shows up during reconciliation, month-end closing, or when someone asks why the books do not match what was actually paid out. This update addresses that exact pain by making payment-related accounting setup more precise from the start.

We improved the payment settings experience so the right assigned accounts can now be filtered based on the type of payment you are working with. In plain language, that means the system is better at showing only the account choices that make sense for the specific transaction in front of you. Instead of sorting through a messy list and second-guessing which account belongs where, your team gets a cleaner and more guided setup flow. That reduces setup mistakes, shortens admin time, and makes the platform feel more dependable when handling real payroll and disbursement work. It is a small-looking change on the surface, but it fixes a very practical workflow that finance and payroll teams deal with every week.

For you, this means cleaner books, fewer avoidable posting errors, and more confidence that each payment is being tracked properly. It also means less back-and-forth between payroll staff and accounting staff just to correct category mismatches after the fact. These kinds of foundational improvements matter because a true backoffice platform is not only about calculating pay correctly, but also about making every downstream finance step smoother. YAHSHUA One keeps moving toward that goal by tightening the connection between payroll actions and accounting clarity. We are continuing to close the gaps that stand between a useful payroll tool and a complete operating system for your business.

title: Theo, Your AI Payroll Copilot, Just Got a Major Brain Upgrade
description: We shipped a massive expansion to Theo, YAHSHUA One's built-in AI assistant. Theo can now help you with nearly every corner of the platform — from running payroll and computing reports, to checking attendance records, looking up compensation details, reviewing compliance and GL settings, and filing employee requests. Theo also gained the ability to accept file uploads, so you can hand it documents directly for processing. Under the hood, Theo now auto-discovers which tools are available, making it smarter and more adaptable as we keep adding features. This is a major step toward a fully conversational payroll experience — where you can just ask, and it gets done.
body: When we first introduced Theo, the idea was simple: your payroll system should be able to answer questions in plain language. Ask it why an employee's deduction changed, and it tells you. Ask it to run payroll for the week, and it does. That original vision held — but the scope was narrow. Theo could only operate on a small slice of the platform. Today, that changes in a big way. We've expanded Theo's toolset to span nearly every module in YAHSHUA One, so no matter where you are in the system, Theo can help you get the job done without switching context or hunting through menus.

Under the hood, the biggest architectural change is that Theo now auto-discovers available tools at runtime. Previously, we had to manually wire up each capability as a hardcoded function. That approach worked early on, but it meant every new platform feature required a separate integration pass before Theo could use it. The new system flips that model: as we build new features into YAHSHUA One, Theo automatically picks them up and knows how to use them. This makes Theo's intelligence scale with the platform itself, rather than lagging behind it. For you, that means fewer gaps — Theo stays current as we ship.

We also added file upload support directly in the Theo chat interface. This was one of the most-requested capabilities from early testers. Now you can hand Theo a document — an employee contract, a government remittance form, an imported CSV — and ask it to process, validate, or extract information from it. Theo handles the heavy lifting and surfaces what matters. Combined with the expanded tool coverage and the auto-discovery system, this update marks a genuine inflection point for how teams will interact with YAHSHUA One. The goal has always been a fully conversational backoffice — where you describe what you need, and it gets done. We're closer to that than ever.

title: Cleaner, Smarter Approvals — Now Fully Role-Based
description: We've cleaned up how approvals and task assignments work under the hood. The old manual assignment system has been fully replaced with a proper role-based access control and approval chain setup. This means who can approve, review, or act on any request is now determined by your company's permission structure — no more one-off manual assignments. As your team grows, approvals scale with it automatically, and everything stays consistent and auditable.

title: Payroll Configuration, Leave Requests & Contribution Reports All Live
description: A huge wave of updates landed today. Your payroll settings have been completely reorganized — everything from rate settings to pay group configurations now lives in one dedicated Payroll Configuration section, making it much easier to find and manage your rules. Employees can now file leave requests directly through the system, with proper approval routing and lifecycle tracking from submission to resolution. Time-based requests (like overtime or schedule adjustments) also now go through a structured filing process. On the reports side, contribution and remittance summaries are live, alongside attendance and daily hours reports and an employee logs monitoring page — giving you a clear view of everything that happened across your workforce in any given period. We also completed a fully configurable payroll computation engine, which means the system can now handle complex, company-specific computation rules rather than one-size-fits-all formulas.

title: Cash Advances Now Fully Managed End-to-End
description: Employee cash advances now have a complete lifecycle inside YAHSHUA One. Your team can submit requests, route them through multi-stage approvals, and choose how repayments are structured — including post-dated check (PDC) disbursements and custom scheduled payment plans with specific dates per installment. Attachments can be made required based on your company policy. Two new payroll report pages also went live: the Payroll Summary Detail and Year-to-Date Tax Summary are now fully accessible, giving you a clear, downloadable picture of what was paid and withheld across any pay period.

title: All Payroll Change Requests Now Have Proper Approvals
description: Every payroll change in YAHSHUA One now goes through the right hands before it takes effect. We completed the full PDC (post-dated change) request and approval system — covering schedule adjustments, recurring deductions, additional pays, and recurring earnings — each with multi-step approval workflows, attachment support, and email notifications built in. Two new report pages are also live: Payroll Summary and Year-to-Date Tax Summary, giving you a clear audit trail of what was paid and withheld across your entire workforce.

title: 13th Month Pay & Final Pay, Now Fully Automated
description: 13th month pay can now be generated directly from your payroll data — the system computes everything automatically based on your employees' actual earnings, no more manual spreadsheets or year-end scrambling. Final pay for departing employees also got a major upgrade: the platform now manages the full lifecycle of a separation — tracking each affected employee, computing their last pay, and handling adjustments on the fly. On top of that, the detailed pay breakdown view keeps getting richer, with new tabs showing exactly how each employee's rate and tax were derived so every peso is fully explained.

title: Final Pay, Employee Schedules & More Payroll Power
description: Big day for payroll. You can now compute Final Pay for departing employees — all the complex stuff like unused leave conversions, prorated pay, and separation scenarios are handled automatically by the system. Recurring work schedules can now be assigned per employee, so the system always knows what hours each person is supposed to work without having to re-enter it every cycle. Workflow configurations now have a dedicated admin section where you can set up, view, and edit your approval processes from one place. The payroll engine itself was also significantly upgraded with more edge-case calculations, moving closer to handling the full range of Filipino payroll scenarios your business will encounter.

title: PhilHealth & SSS Remittance Files, Ready to Submit
description: You can now export your PhilHealth and SSS government remittance files directly from YAHSHUA One — in the exact CSV format that the agencies require, complete with the correct column headers (MEM_PHIC, MO_SAL, EE_STAT for PhilHealth; EE/ER/EC/MPF breakdown for SSS). No more manually building spreadsheets or reformatting data before submission. Just run your payroll, generate the file, and submit. This brings government contribution compliance from a manual chore to a one-click task.

title: Face Recognition, Pay History & Full Payroll Engines
description: YAHSHUA One can now identify employees by their face — enabling biometric clock-ins and image-based attendance lookups directly from your admin dashboard, no manual searching required. We also completed a full set of complex payroll calculation engines: night differential, back pay, longevity pay, half-day leave deductions, double holiday rates, and previous-month government contribution carryovers are all now computed automatically. On top of that, every employee now has a full salary history — every rate change, with dates and audit trail — and bulk overtime or work requests can be imported straight from a CSV instead of entered one by one.

title: Full Payroll Breakdown — See Every Cent Explained
description: You can now open any payroll line item and see a full, transparent breakdown of exactly how each employee's pay was calculated — rate derivation, government contributions with bracket matching, annualized tax computation, and the exact payroll settings that were active on their pay period. No more guessing or calling your accountant to explain a deduction. Everything is clear, organized tab by tab. We also completed the help management system for platform administrators, paving the way for in-app guidance across YAHSHUA One.

title: Smarter Timesheet Verification & Attendance Reviews
description: Timesheet verification just got a major upgrade. The system now checks attendance record by record, per employee, and automatically flags anything suspicious — like overtime logged on a rest day or hours that don't add up — with a red badge so your team never misses an issue. Locked attendance periods can now be unlocked with a password when corrections are needed, and a full activity log tracks every change. Reviews now follow a clean three-stage flow (Reviewer → Recommender → Approver) with live progress bars showing exactly where things stand. You can also add remarks to any individual attendance record, and bulk-approve or bulk-review all records in one click.

title: Daily Product Improvements
description: We improved account access flows so getting into YAHSHUA One is smoother and more reliable. We also tightened communication workflows so product updates reach users more consistently.

title: Approvals and User Controls Expanded
description: Yesterday we completed a major upgrade to approvals and employee requests: teams can now run cleaner multi-step review flows, track leave conversions end to end, and process bulk profile updates with better visibility. We also expanded user management and reporting controls while tightening permissions and data checks, so day-to-day HR and payroll operations are safer and more reliable for growing Filipino companies.

title: Landing Page Refresh + SEO Foundations Now Live
description: We upgraded the YAHSHUA One landing experience with a cleaner visual system, stronger brand styling, and better readability across sections. We also added core SEO foundations — richer metadata, structured data, canonical tags, robots/sitemap support, and social preview setup — so the site is easier to discover and share as we keep shipping product updates.

title: Payroll Settings Now Fully Configurable
description: The team completed the full payroll settings module — you can now configure every rule that governs how your employees get paid: deduction priorities, leave conversion, net pay rounding, and automatic allowances or deductions by location and employment type. Government contribution rules (SSS, PhilHealth, Pag-IBIG) are now fully customizable down to the employee level, and rate settings let you set night differential windows, pre-shift rates, and flexible pay overrides by department or position. Shift management and multi-level allowance approvals are also now fully built and ready to use.

title: Recurring Pay, Gov't Contributions & Built-in Help
description: Managing your employees' pay just got a lot more powerful. You can now set up recurring earnings — like regular allowances — per employee, with full history, audit trails, and linked deduction support so everything stays in sync. Government contributions (SSS, PhilHealth, Pag-IBIG) are now fully customizable: override amounts per employee, set effectivity dates, apply per-location rules, and track exactly which rates apply at any given time. We also built the foundation for a built-in help system — so contextual guidance will be available right where you need it, without ever leaving the platform.

title: Dependents, Cost Center History & Smarter Work Schedules
description: Employee profiles got a serious upgrade today. You can now record employee dependents directly from their profile page, track each employee's cost center history, and set up custom employee ID formats and auto-numbering for your company. Work schedule configurations now cascade automatically — set it once at the top level and it flows down, so your payroll always picks up the right schedule without manual updates. Regularization reports are also live, giving you a clear view of which probationary employees are approaching their regularization date. Organizational data like locations, departments, employment types, and sections now each have their own dedicated pages, making your company structure much easier to manage. You can also bulk-import divisions and remove sections or units you no longer need.

title: BIR Forms, Org Structure & 13th Month Pay Live
description: Yesterday was a massive day. Your organizational chart is now fully supported in YAHSHUA One — employees can be assigned to sections, units, and sub-units with full history tracking, so payroll always knows exactly who belongs where and when. BIR Forms 2316 and 1604C are now built in, letting you generate government-required tax certificates directly from the platform. The 13th Month Pay report is live too — view per-employee breakdowns, export to Excel, manage adjustments, and recalculate anytime. Cost center tracking and timesheet verification also got wired up and ready.

title: Employee Profiles, Encryption & Cleaner Navigation
description: Employee management got another round of polish today. You can now view and edit personal information directly from an employee's profile, including for inactive or previously separated employees. The 'Add Employee' button now has a cleaner dropdown, and bulk updates display selected employees more clearly. Employee email addresses are now stored encrypted for better data security. The payroll sidebar navigation was also cleaned up, making it easier to find what you're looking for.

title: Staff Portal, Employee Editing & Government Reports
description: A major push landed today across three areas. First, a full internal staff portal is now live — the YAHSHUA One team can manage companies, users, subscriptions, billing, and audit logs in one place, with proper role-based access and secure account switching between companies. Second, employee management got a big upgrade: you can now edit personal information directly from an employee's profile, view profiles of inactive or separated employees, and bulk-update employees with clearer selections and a faster refresh flow. Third, government contribution reports are now fully available — SSS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG summaries, 13th month pay calculations, and year-to-date tax reports can all be generated from the payroll module. Post-dated check (PDC) requests also now flow through proper approval stages.

title: Approvals, Payroll Exports & AI Engine Live
description: You can now set up multi-stage approval workflows for employee data changes — every update goes through Review, Recommend, and Approve stages before it takes effect, with full visibility into who approved what and when. Payroll registers now export directly to bank disbursement formats (RCBC, Metrobank, BDO), and payslips can be emailed to your entire team in one click. Under the hood, the AI copilot engine is now fully wired in — laying the foundation for the smart automation features coming your way.

title: YAHSHUA One is Now in Development
description: We officially kicked off building YAHSHUA One — your all-in-one backoffice platform. The foundation is set: payroll, accounting, and HR modules are underway. We are building in public and will keep you updated every step of the way.
